title: Acme review decision (the A/R/E learning ledger)
description: One human approve/revise/reject/hold decision, appended (newest last) to output/engine/decisions.jsonl by approvals.py via engine.record_decision(). Append-only; never rewritten. Captures the CONTENT each decision judged so the system learns which prompts/scripts/angles get approved vs rejected. This is the SUPABASE-MIGRATION CONTRACT: each line maps 1:1 to a row of the `review_decisions` table (8th table alongside Devon's 7; see MIGRATION.md §1A.5 data layer). Local JSONL is the system-of-record during the build; db.py (supabase-py) replays/streams these rows into Postgres at cutover — there is no other path for the review history to reach Supabase.

job_id.description: FK -> the job; Postgres: text, indexed.

verb.description: The human decision. reject/revise are the negative signals the feedback loop steers away from.

gate.description: concept = F7 reel pre-credit script/concept gate; final = the publish sign-off gate.

who.description: Telegram username / attribution of the reviewer.

note.description: The reviewer's REASON (free text). The most valuable learning signal on a rejection.

content.description: Snapshot of what was judged (only non-empty fields are kept). Postgres: a jsonb column, or normalized into review_decisions columns. This is the corpus rejected_lessons()/rejected_topics() mine.

type.description: Content kind — scopes the feedback (reel lessons -> script.py, image -> research.py).

topic.description: The angle/topic. rejected_topics() avoids re-proposing a hard-rejected one.

script.description: REEL: the spoken script (RV2). The thing a rejected-reel lesson steers script.py away from.

video_prompts.description: REEL: the b-roll generation prompts (the only Higgsfield-credit spend).

bg_prompt.description: IMAGE: background generation prompt, when bg_policy=generate.

caption_x.description: The X caption that was judged.
